chapter_summary: Jesus tells three stories about things that were lost and then found — a sheep, a silver coin, and a son. Each story shows how much God loves every person and how heaven celebrates when someone comes back to Him.
main_lesson: Jesus never stops looking for us when we are lost, and heaven celebrates when we come back to God.

memory_verse.kjv: I say unto you, that likewise joy shall be in heaven over one sinner that repenteth, more than over ninety and nine just persons, which need no repentance.
memory_verse.little_bible: Jesus says that all of heaven is filled with joy — more joy than you can imagine — when even one person turns back to God.

parent_guide: Luke 15 contains three of Jesus' most beloved parables, spoken in response to the Pharisees' criticism that He ate with sinners (vv1-2). The Lost Sheep (vv1-7), Lost Coin (vv8-10), and Lost Son (vv11-32) all answer the same question: Why does Jesus welcome sinners? Because God actively seeks the lost and throws a celebration when they return. The prodigal son story is rich enough for multiple conversations — the younger son's repentance, the father's extravagant grace, and the older son's self-righteous anger are all worth exploring with children of different ages.
application_for_children: No matter how far you've wandered or how many mistakes you've made, God is watching for you and will run to meet you when you come back to Him.

verses.kjv: Then drew near unto him all the publicans and sinners for to hear him.
verses.little_bible: Tax collectors and people who had done wrong things all crowded close to Jesus because they wanted to hear what He was saying.
verses.little_reader_adaptation: People who had made mistakes came close to hear Jesus.
verses.meaning: The very people society rejected were drawn to Jesus — showing that He welcomed those who knew they needed help.
verses.memory_phrase: Everyone is welcome near Jesus.
verses.prayer: Jesus, thank You that You welcome everyone who comes to You, even me.
verses.discussion_question: Why do you think the people who had made mistakes wanted to be near Jesus?
verses.family_discussion: Have you ever felt like you were not good enough to come to God? What does this verse tell us?
verses.do_it_today: Invite someone who usually sits alone to come sit near you today.
verses.illustration_prompt: A warm crowd of diverse people pressing close to listen to Jesus, who stands with an open and welcoming expression, storybook watercolor style.

verses.kjv: And the Pharisees and scribes murmured, saying, This man receiveth sinners, and eateth with them.
verses.little_bible: But the religious leaders were upset. They whispered to each other, 'Look at Jesus — He spends time with bad people and even eats with them!'
verses.little_reader_adaptation: Some leaders were unhappy. 'Why does Jesus eat with bad people?' they said.
verses.meaning: The Pharisees saw Jesus' welcome of sinners as scandalous — but Jesus was showing that God's love reaches those who are most broken.
verses.memory_phrase: Jesus eats with sinners.
verses.prayer: God, help me not to look down on anyone. Help me love people the way Jesus does.
verses.discussion_question: Why do you think the religious leaders were bothered by Jesus spending time with sinners?
verses.family_discussion: Is it easy or hard to be kind to people others don't like? How can Jesus help us do that?
verses.do_it_today: Eat your next meal thinking about someone you find it hard to be kind to, and pray for them.
verses.illustration_prompt: Pharisees in long robes whispering and frowning in the background while Jesus laughs warmly with a group of people at a shared table, storybook watercolor style.

verses.kjv: And he spake this parable unto them, saying,
verses.little_bible: So Jesus told them a story.
verses.little_reader_adaptation: Then Jesus told them a story.
verses.meaning: Jesus responded to criticism not with argument but with stories — because stories open hearts and help us see truth in a new way.
verses.memory_phrase: Jesus tells stories to teach us.
verses.prayer: Jesus, help me to listen carefully to Your stories and learn from them.
verses.discussion_question: Why do you think Jesus told stories to explain things?
verses.family_discussion: What is your favourite story Jesus told, and what did it teach you?
verses.do_it_today: Tell a younger child or sibling a story that teaches something good.
verses.illustration_prompt: Jesus sitting among a crowd under an open sky, everyone leaning in to listen, an air of expectation, storybook watercolor style.

verses.kjv: What man of you, having an hundred sheep, if he lose one of them, doth not leave the ninety and nine in the wilderness, and go after that which is lost, until he find it?
verses.little_bible: Jesus asked, 'If you had a hundred sheep and one got lost, wouldn't you leave the other ninety-nine and go searching until you found the missing one?'
verses.little_reader_adaptation: If one sheep got lost, a good shepherd goes to find it.
verses.meaning: God is like a shepherd who does not give up on even one lost person — every single soul matters infinitely to Him.
verses.memory_phrase: God searches for every lost one.
verses.prayer: God, thank You that You would leave everything to come and find me.
verses.discussion_question: If you were the shepherd, how would you feel about the one lost sheep?
verses.family_discussion: What does it mean to you that God would leave the ninety-nine to find you?
verses.do_it_today: Draw a picture of a shepherd carrying a sheep on his shoulders.
verses.illustration_prompt: A shepherd setting off alone into rugged hills at dusk, lantern in hand, searching for a small lost sheep, storybook watercolor style.

verses.kjv: And when he hath found it, he layeth it on his shoulders, rejoicing.
verses.little_bible: When the shepherd finally finds the lost sheep, he picks it up and carries it on his shoulders, full of joy.
verses.little_reader_adaptation: When he finds the sheep, he carries it home with great joy.
verses.meaning: The shepherd does not scold the sheep — he carries it home with joy. This is how God treats us when we return to Him.
verses.memory_phrase: He carries us home with joy.
verses.prayer: Jesus, thank You for carrying me when I am lost and tired.
verses.discussion_question: How do you think the sheep felt being carried on the shepherd's shoulders?
verses.family_discussion: When have you felt like God carried you through something hard?
verses.do_it_today: Give someone in your family a big hug and tell them you're glad they're home.
verses.illustration_prompt: A beaming shepherd with a fluffy sheep draped joyfully across his shoulders, walking home through golden light, storybook watercolor style.

verses.kjv: And when he cometh home, he calleth together his friends and neighbours, saying unto them, Rejoice with me; for I have found my sheep which was lost.
verses.little_bible: Back home, the shepherd gathers all his friends and neighbours and shouts, 'Celebrate with me! I found my lost sheep!'
verses.little_reader_adaptation: He calls his friends and says, 'Celebrate! I found my lost sheep!'
verses.meaning: Finding what was lost is so wonderful that it calls for a party — God's joy over a returning sinner overflows into celebration.
verses.memory_phrase: Share the joy of being found.
verses.prayer: God, let me share Your joy and celebrate when people come back to You.
verses.discussion_question: Why did the shepherd want to share his happy news with everyone?
verses.family_discussion: How can your family celebrate when someone turns back to God?
verses.do_it_today: Tell someone good news today — share something that made you happy.
verses.illustration_prompt: A cheerful gathering outside a shepherd's cottage, neighbours clapping and smiling, the shepherd holding up his lamb proudly, storybook watercolor style.

verses.kjv: I say unto you, that likewise joy shall be in heaven over one sinner that repenteth, more than over ninety and nine just persons, which need no repentance.
verses.little_bible: Jesus said, 'I am telling you the truth — all of heaven throws a party when even one person says sorry to God and turns back to Him. That is more wonderful than anything!'
verses.little_reader_adaptation: Heaven is full of joy when one person comes back to God.
verses.meaning: Heaven's greatest celebration is not for perfect people but for those who were lost and choose to return — repentance is precious to God.
verses.memory_phrase: Heaven rejoices over one who repents.
verses.prayer: God, I want to make heaven celebrate. Help me to always turn back to You when I go wrong.
verses.discussion_question: Does it surprise you that all of heaven celebrates for just one person? Why?
verses.family_discussion: What does repentance mean, and why do you think it brings such great joy in heaven?
verses.do_it_today: Say a quiet sorry to God for one thing you've done wrong this week, and receive His forgiveness.
verses.illustration_prompt: A glorious scene of angels and heavenly figures celebrating with light and joy, radiating outward from a single bright figure below, storybook watercolor style.

verses.kjv: Either what woman having ten pieces of silver, if she lose one piece, doth not light a candle, and sweep the house, and seek diligently till she find it?
verses.little_bible: Jesus asked another question: 'If a woman had ten silver coins and lost one, wouldn't she light a lamp and sweep every corner of the house until she found it?'
verses.little_reader_adaptation: A woman lost a coin and searched her whole house to find it.
verses.meaning: Even a single coin — small and easy to overlook — is searched for with great effort, showing how much each person matters to God.
verses.memory_phrase: God searches every corner for you.
verses.prayer: God, thank You that I matter enough for You to search for me.
verses.discussion_question: Why would someone search so hard for just one coin?
verses.family_discussion: What does this story tell you about how much value God places on every person?
verses.do_it_today: Help tidy one room in your home today — and as you do, think about how God searches for us.
verses.illustration_prompt: A woman in a cosy home holding a lamp close to the floor, sweeping and searching earnestly in every corner, storybook watercolor style.

verses.kjv: And when she hath found it, she calleth her friends and her neighbours together, saying, Rejoice with me; for I have found the piece which I had lost.
verses.little_bible: When she finally finds the coin, she calls her friends and neighbours and says, 'Come celebrate with me! I found the coin I had lost!'
verses.little_reader_adaptation: She finds it and calls her friends: 'Celebrate with me!'
verses.meaning: Joy that cannot be contained — when we are found by God, it is news worth sharing with everyone around us.
verses.memory_phrase: Found! Share the celebration.
verses.prayer: God, help me to be so happy about Your love that I want to share it with everyone.
verses.discussion_question: Why do you think she wanted to share her happiness with her friends?
verses.family_discussion: When has your family celebrated something so good you had to tell others?
verses.do_it_today: Share one thing God has done for you with a friend or family member today.
verses.illustration_prompt: A beaming woman holding up a glinting silver coin to her smiling neighbours at her doorstep, warm lantern light spilling out, storybook watercolor style.

verses.kjv: Likewise, I say unto you, there is joy in the presence of the angels of God over one sinner that repenteth.
verses.little_bible: Jesus said, 'In the same way, all the angels of God are full of joy when one person turns back to God.'
verses.little_reader_adaptation: All God's angels are full of joy when one person comes back to God.
verses.meaning: Angels rejoice over repentance — every returning sinner is a cause of heavenly celebration, reminding us of the infinite value God places on each soul.
verses.memory_phrase: Angels rejoice when you come back to God.
verses.prayer: God, I want the angels to celebrate. Help me always come back to You.
verses.discussion_question: How does it feel to know that angels celebrate because of you?
verses.family_discussion: Why do you think repentance — saying sorry and turning back — makes heaven so happy?
verses.do_it_today: Pray for one person you know who doesn't yet know Jesus, that they would come back to God.
verses.illustration_prompt: A host of joyful angels with radiant faces celebrating in the clouds, one bright light below representing a person turning to God, storybook watercolor style.

verses.kjv: And he said, A certain man had two sons:
verses.little_bible: Then Jesus told one more story. He said, 'There was a man who had two sons.'
verses.little_reader_adaptation: A man had two sons.
verses.meaning: Jesus begins the most detailed and personal of the three parables — a story about family, failure, grace, and love.
verses.memory_phrase: A father had two sons.
verses.prayer: Jesus, help me to listen to this story with an open heart.
verses.discussion_question: Do you have brothers or sisters? How do you treat each other?
verses.family_discussion: What do you think this story might be about just from this opening?
verses.do_it_today: Do something kind for a sibling or a close friend today before they ask.
verses.illustration_prompt: A warm farmhouse setting at dusk, a kind-faced father standing with two boys of different ages, storybook watercolor style.

verses.kjv: And the younger of them said to his father, Father, give me the portion of goods that falleth to me. And he divided unto them his living.
verses.little_bible: The younger son said to his father, 'Dad, give me my share of everything you will leave me one day.' So the father divided his money and property between them.
verses.little_reader_adaptation: The younger son said, 'Give me my share now.' So the father shared everything.
verses.meaning: Asking for an inheritance while the father was still alive was deeply disrespectful — it was as if the son wished his father were dead. Yet the father gave it willingly.
verses.memory_phrase: The son asked for what was not yet his.
verses.prayer: God, help me to be grateful for what I have and not to be greedy.
verses.discussion_question: Do you think it was a good idea for the younger son to ask for his money early? Why?
verses.family_discussion: What does real gratitude look like in our family? How can we show it more?
verses.do_it_today: Thank your parents or carers for something specific they do for you every day.
verses.illustration_prompt: A younger son standing before his father with his hand out, the father's face showing love mixed with sadness as he opens a chest of coins, storybook watercolor style.

verses.kjv: And not many days after the younger son gathered all together, and took his journey into a far country, and there wasted his substance with riotous living.
verses.little_bible: Not long after that, the younger son packed everything up and went far away. In that faraway place, he spent all his money carelessly on wild living.
verses.little_reader_adaptation: The son went far away and wasted all his money.
verses.meaning: Sin takes us far from home and promises great fun — but it always leads to waste and emptiness.
verses.memory_phrase: He wasted it all far from home.
verses.prayer: God, help me not to be foolish with the gifts You give me.
verses.discussion_question: What do you think 'far away from home' might mean for us when it comes to God?
verses.family_discussion: How does living away from God's ways lead to emptiness? Can you think of examples?
verses.do_it_today: Think of one gift God has given you — your time, your kindness, your energy — and use it well today.
verses.illustration_prompt: A young man with a large bag walking down a long road away from a farmhouse, bright lights of a city ahead of him, storybook watercolor style.

verses.kjv: And when he had spent all, there arose a mighty famine in that land; and he began to be in want.
verses.little_bible: When the son had spent every last coin, a terrible food shortage hit the whole country. He had nothing — no food, no money, no help.
verses.little_reader_adaptation: When his money ran out, there was no food and he was hungry.
verses.meaning: Sin leaves us with nothing — and the world cannot fill the emptiness that only God can fill.
verses.memory_phrase: He had nothing left.
verses.prayer: God, remind me that only You can truly fill my heart. Help me not to look for happiness in the wrong places.
verses.discussion_question: How do you think the young man felt when all his money was gone and there was no food?
verses.family_discussion: What are some things people try to fill their hearts with instead of God? How does that work out?
verses.do_it_today: Donate something you own — a toy or food — to someone who is in need.
verses.illustration_prompt: A young man sitting alone in a dusty, barren landscape with empty pockets, looking hollow and hungry, storybook watercolor style.

verses.kjv: And he went and joined himself to a citizen of that country; and he sent him into his fields to feed swine.
verses.little_bible: Desperate, he found work with a man in that country who sent him to look after pigs in the fields.
verses.little_reader_adaptation: He got a job feeding pigs in a field.
verses.meaning: For a Jewish young man, feeding pigs was the lowest possible work — he had fallen as far as he could go.
verses.memory_phrase: He fell to the lowest place.
verses.prayer: God, when I am at my lowest, help me to remember You are never far away.
verses.discussion_question: Why do you think being a pig feeder was such a sad job for this young man?
verses.family_discussion: Has there been a time in your life when you felt like you had hit rock bottom? How did God meet you there?
verses.do_it_today: Help with a chore you normally avoid — even the smallest job done with love matters to God.
verses.illustration_prompt: A ragged young man hunched in a muddy pig pen, surrounded by pigs, looking ashamed and exhausted, storybook watercolor style.

verses.kjv: And he would fain have filled his belly with the husks that the swine did eat: and no man gave unto him.
verses.little_bible: He was so hungry he wanted to eat the scraps the pigs were eating — but nobody gave him anything.
verses.little_reader_adaptation: He was so hungry he wanted pig food. No one helped him.
verses.meaning: The boy was utterly alone, forgotten, and starving — sin had taken everything and left him with nothing and no one.
verses.memory_phrase: No one helped him.
verses.prayer: God, help me never to forget people who are hungry, lonely, or forgotten.
verses.discussion_question: Can you imagine being so hungry you wanted to eat pig food? How desperate must he have felt?
verses.family_discussion: How should we respond when we see people who are truly in need around us?
verses.do_it_today: Pray for one person who is lonely or struggling today. If you can, do something kind for them.
verses.illustration_prompt: A gaunt young man staring sadly at a trough of pig food in a grey field, completely alone, storybook watercolor style.

verses.kjv: And when he came to himself, he said, How many hired servants of my father's have bread enough and to spare, and I perish with hunger!
verses.little_bible: Then it was like he woke up and thought clearly for the first time. He said to himself, 'Even my father's servants have plenty to eat — and here I am, starving to death!'
verses.little_reader_adaptation: He thought, 'Even my father's workers have more food than me!'
verses.meaning: The turning point — he 'came to himself,' which is what repentance looks like: waking up to reality and remembering who you truly are and where home is.
verses.memory_phrase: He came back to himself.
verses.prayer: God, help me to wake up quickly when I am going the wrong way, and to remember where home is.
verses.discussion_question: What do you think changed in the young man's heart when he 'came to himself'?
verses.family_discussion: What does it look like to 'come to yourself' spiritually — to wake up and realize you've been going the wrong way?
verses.do_it_today: Spend two quiet minutes thinking about whether there is anything in your life you need to turn back from.
verses.illustration_prompt: A young man in a pig field with a sudden change on his face — eyes wide open, as if waking from a dream, a distant farmhouse visible on the horizon, storybook watercolor style.

verses.kjv: I will arise and go to my father, and will say unto him, Father, I have sinned against heaven, and before thee,
verses.little_bible: He made up his mind: 'I will get up, go home to my father, and tell him — Father, I have done wrong against God and against you.'
verses.little_reader_adaptation: He said, 'I will go home and tell my father I was wrong.'
verses.meaning: True repentance leads to a decision and a direction — he chose to arise and go, and he prepared to confess honestly.
verses.memory_phrase: I will arise and go home.
verses.prayer: God, give me the courage to say I am sorry and to come home to You.
verses.discussion_question: Why is it important to say sorry with your words, not just feel sorry inside?
verses.family_discussion: What does it mean to sin against God and against another person at the same time?
verses.do_it_today: If there is someone you need to say sorry to, say it today — out loud, with your words.
verses.illustration_prompt: A young man standing up in a muddy field, face set with determination, eyes looking toward the distant horizon where home lies, storybook watercolor style.

verses.kjv: And am no more worthy to be called thy son: make me as one of thy hired servants.
verses.little_bible: He planned to say, 'I am not good enough to be called your son anymore. Please just let me be one of your workers.'
verses.little_reader_adaptation: He thought, 'I am not good enough to be his son. I will ask to be a worker.'
verses.meaning: Genuine humility — he did not demand anything, only hoped for mercy. True repentance always comes with a humble heart.
verses.memory_phrase: He came home with a humble heart.
verses.prayer: God, help me to be humble and honest when I come to You, not pretending I deserve anything.
verses.discussion_question: Why did the son think he was no longer worthy to be called a son?
verses.family_discussion: What is the difference between a servant and a son? How does God treat us when we come back to Him?
verses.do_it_today: Do one act of service for someone without expecting anything in return.
verses.illustration_prompt: A young man walking along a dusty road, head slightly bowed, rehearsing his words quietly, a humble and hopeful expression on his face, storybook watercolor style.

verses.kjv: And he arose, and came to his father. But when he was yet a great way off, his father saw him, and had compassion, and ran, and fell on his neck, and kissed him.
verses.little_bible: So he got up and started the long walk home. But while he was still far away, his father spotted him. His father's heart was bursting with love — he ran all the way to his son, threw his arms around him, and kissed him.
verses.little_reader_adaptation: His father saw him far away and ran to him with a big hug.
verses.meaning: The father was watching and waiting — when the son returned, the father ran. This is the most powerful picture of God's grace in all of Scripture.
verses.memory_phrase: The father ran to meet him.
verses.prayer: God, thank You for running to meet me when I come back to You. Your love is amazing.
verses.discussion_question: Why do you think the father ran? What does that tell us about God?
verses.family_discussion: God was watching for the son while he was still far off. What does that tell us about how God watches for us?
verses.do_it_today: Run to greet someone you love today — show them with your whole body that you are glad they are here.
verses.illustration_prompt: A joyful father running with his robes flying, arms wide open, toward a ragged young man on the dusty road, tears streaming down the father's face, golden afternoon light, storybook watercolor style.

verses.kjv: And the son said unto him, Father, I have sinned against heaven, and in thy sight, and am no more worthy to be called thy son.
verses.little_bible: The son said, 'Dad, I have done wrong against God and against you. I am not good enough to be your son anymore.'
verses.little_reader_adaptation: The son said, 'Father, I have sinned. I am not worthy to be your son.'
verses.meaning: The son gave his prepared confession — honest, humble, and complete. He did not make excuses or blame others.
verses.memory_phrase: He said sorry from his heart.
verses.prayer: God, help me to say sorry honestly, without making excuses, just like the son did.
verses.discussion_question: The son told his dad the truth even though it was hard. Is it ever hard for you to say sorry honestly?
verses.family_discussion: What makes a real apology different from one that tries to cover things up?
verses.do_it_today: Think about whether you have been making excuses for something. Choose honesty instead.
verses.illustration_prompt: A son kneeling or bowing his head before his father on the road, speaking with an earnest and remorseful expression, storybook watercolor style.

verses.kjv: But the father said to his servants, Bring forth the best robe, and put it on him; and put a ring on his hand, and shoes on his feet:
verses.little_bible: But before the son could say anything more, his father called out to his servants: 'Bring the best robe and put it on him! Put a ring on his finger and shoes on his feet!'
verses.little_reader_adaptation: The father called, 'Give him the best robe, a ring, and shoes!'
verses.meaning: The father did not scold or lecture — he immediately restored the son to full dignity. The robe, ring, and shoes are signs of being a beloved child, not a servant.
verses.memory_phrase: He restored him completely.
verses.prayer: God, thank You that You don't just forgive me — You fully restore me and call me Your child.
verses.discussion_question: Why do you think the father gave his son the best robe and a ring? What was he saying?
verses.family_discussion: How does God's forgiveness do more than just wipe away the bad? How does He restore us?
verses.do_it_today: Give someone a special gift or treat today just to show them they are valued and loved.
verses.illustration_prompt: Servants draping a beautiful robe around a stunned but grateful young man, the father beaming with delight as a ring is placed on his son's finger, storybook watercolor style.

verses.kjv: And bring hither the fatted calf, and kill it; and let us eat, and be merry:
verses.little_bible: The father also said, 'Bring the best calf we have been saving and prepare a feast — we are going to eat and celebrate!'
verses.little_reader_adaptation: The father said, 'Prepare a big feast! We are going to celebrate!'
verses.meaning: The father ordered the very best for a celebration — God's welcome is not reluctant or half-hearted but extravagantly joyful.
verses.memory_phrase: Let us eat and celebrate!
verses.prayer: God, thank You for celebrating me. Help me to celebrate others too.
verses.discussion_question: What is the biggest celebration your family has ever had? How did it feel?
verses.family_discussion: How does this picture of a feast help us understand what it means to come home to God?
verses.do_it_today: Help plan or prepare something special for your family tonight — a favourite meal or a simple celebration.
verses.illustration_prompt: A busy farmyard with servants preparing a joyful feast, fires lit, tables being laid, a sense of excited bustle, storybook watercolor style.

verses.kjv: For this my son was dead, and is alive again; he was lost, and is found. And they began to be merry.
verses.little_bible: The father said, 'My son was as good as dead — and now he is alive again! He was lost — and now he is found!' And the celebration began.
verses.little_reader_adaptation: The father said, 'My son was lost, and now he is found!' And the party began.
verses.meaning: Coming back to God is like coming back to life — repentance and return are resurrection, from death to life, from lost to found.
verses.memory_phrase: Lost and found. Dead and alive again.
verses.prayer: God, thank You for making me alive when I was lost. Help me never forget what You did for me.
verses.discussion_question: How is coming back to God like coming back to life?
verses.family_discussion: Have you ever experienced something like this — a time when you felt far from God and then came back?
verses.do_it_today: Write or draw one thing God has brought back to life in your heart.
verses.illustration_prompt: A father with hands raised in joyful proclamation, friends and servants beginning to dance and celebrate around a returning son, warm firelight, storybook watercolor style.

verses.kjv: Now his elder son was in the field: and as he came and drew nigh to the house, he heard musick and dancing.
verses.little_bible: Meanwhile, the older brother had been out working in the fields. As he walked back toward the house, he heard music and dancing.
verses.little_reader_adaptation: The older brother came home and heard music and dancing.
verses.meaning: The older son represents those who serve faithfully but miss the heart of the Father — performance without joy.
verses.memory_phrase: He heard music but did not understand.
verses.prayer: God, help me to understand Your joy, not just follow Your rules.
verses.discussion_question: What do you think the older brother felt when he heard the music?
verses.family_discussion: Is it possible to do the right things but have the wrong heart? What does that look like?
verses.do_it_today: Join in something joyful today — sing a song, dance, or laugh with your family.
verses.illustration_prompt: An older young man pausing on a path, a hoe over his shoulder, head tilted toward a lit-up farmhouse where music can be heard, a cautious expression on his face, storybook watercolor style.

verses.kjv: And he called one of the servants, and asked what these things meant.
verses.little_bible: He stopped and asked one of the servants, 'What is going on in there?'
verses.little_reader_adaptation: He asked a servant, 'What is happening?'
verses.meaning: Rather than going inside, he stopped to ask — he was uncertain and guarded where he should have been eager and joyful.
verses.memory_phrase: He asked instead of rejoicing.
verses.prayer: God, when good things happen to others, help me to be happy for them too.
verses.discussion_question: Why do you think the older brother stopped to ask instead of just going in?
verses.family_discussion: Is it ever hard to celebrate when good things happen to someone else? What makes it hard?
verses.do_it_today: Be genuinely happy for someone else's good news today — congratulate them warmly.
verses.illustration_prompt: An older son standing stiffly at the gate, speaking to a servant, the bright lights and sounds of celebration spilling from the house behind, storybook watercolor style.

verses.kjv: And he said unto him, Thy brother is come; and thy father hath killed the fatted calf, because he hath received him safe and sound.
verses.little_bible: The servant told him, 'Your brother has come home! Your father has thrown a big feast because he came back safe and well.'
verses.little_reader_adaptation: The servant said, 'Your brother is home! Your father is celebrating!'
verses.meaning: The servant's words are full of good news — but the older son cannot yet hear it as good.
verses.memory_phrase: Your brother has come home.
verses.prayer: God, help me to see others' homecoming as good news, just like You do.
verses.discussion_question: How do you think the older brother felt when he heard his younger brother was back?
verses.family_discussion: Why might it be hard for the older brother to feel happy about his brother's return?
verses.do_it_today: Reach out to someone you have grown distant from — send them a message or say hello.
verses.illustration_prompt: A servant with a warm and happy expression explaining to a stiff older brother outside, sounds of celebration drifting from the open door, storybook watercolor style.

verses.kjv: And he was angry, and would not go in: therefore came his father out, and intreated him.
verses.little_bible: The older brother became angry and refused to go inside. So his father came out to him and gently begged him to come in.
verses.little_reader_adaptation: The older brother was angry and would not go in. So the father came out to him.
verses.meaning: The father did not ignore the older son's anger — he came out to him, just as he had run to the younger. God pursues both the wanderer and the resentful.
verses.memory_phrase: The father came out to him too.
verses.prayer: God, thank You that You come out to find me even when I am angry or far away in my heart.
verses.discussion_question: Was the older brother right to be angry? Why or why not?
verses.family_discussion: Have you ever felt like the older brother — working hard but feeling unnoticed? How did that feel?
verses.do_it_today: If you are feeling left out or upset, tell someone you trust how you are feeling instead of staying outside.
verses.illustration_prompt: A father stepping outside into the cool evening, arms open, approaching an older son who stands with crossed arms and a furrowed brow, storybook watercolor style.

verses.kjv: And he answering said to his father, Lo, these many years do I serve thee, neither transgressed I at any time thy commandment: and yet thou never gavest me a kid, that I might make merry with my friends:
verses.little_bible: The older son said, 'Father, I have worked for you all these years without ever disobeying you. But you have never even given me a small goat so I could celebrate with my friends!'
verses.little_reader_adaptation: He said, 'I have worked so hard for you. But you never gave me a party!'
verses.meaning: The older son was keeping score — but he had been treating his father as an employer, not a loving dad, and had missed the joy of relationship all along.
verses.memory_phrase: He kept score instead of enjoying love.
verses.prayer: God, help me not to serve You to earn things, but because I love You and You love me.
verses.discussion_question: Do you ever feel like you are not being treated fairly compared to someone else? How do you handle it?
verses.family_discussion: What is the difference between obeying God because you love Him and obeying just to earn rewards?
verses.do_it_today: Do something kind for your family today without keeping track of whether it gets noticed.
verses.illustration_prompt: An older son gesturing emphatically with his hands, making his case to a patient father who listens with gentle sadness, storybook watercolor style.

verses.kjv: But as soon as this thy son was come, which hath devoured thy living with harlots, thou hast killed for him the fatted calf.
verses.little_bible: He continued, 'But the moment this son of yours shows up — the one who threw away your money on wicked living — you kill the best calf for him!'
verses.little_reader_adaptation: He said, 'Your other son wasted your money, and you throw him a party!'
verses.meaning: The older son's anger reveals bitterness and a refusal to call his brother 'my brother' — he said 'this son of yours.' Unforgiveness separates us from love.
verses.memory_phrase: Bitterness keeps us outside.
verses.prayer: God, help me not to let bitterness grow in my heart. Help me to forgive like You do.
verses.discussion_question: Why do you think the older son said 'this son of yours' instead of 'my brother'?
verses.family_discussion: How does holding onto bitterness hurt us as much as it hurts others? How can we let it go?
verses.do_it_today: Think of someone you might be holding a grudge against. Ask God to help you forgive them.
verses.illustration_prompt: An older son pointing accusingly toward the lit window of the house, face showing resentment, the father listening patiently, storybook watercolor style.

verses.kjv: And he said unto him, Son, thou art ever with me, and all that I have is thine.
verses.little_bible: The father replied softly, 'My son, you have been with me all this time — and everything I have is already yours.'
verses.little_reader_adaptation: The father said, 'Son, you are always with me, and all I have is yours.'
verses.meaning: The older son had been living in the father's house without enjoying the riches of relationship — grace was always available to him too.
verses.memory_phrase: All that I have is yours.
verses.prayer: God, help me to enjoy being with You, not just to work for You.
verses.discussion_question: What do you think the older son had forgotten about living in his father's house?
verses.family_discussion: How can we enjoy our relationship with God every day, not just when we need something?
verses.do_it_today: Spend five minutes just being with God — not asking for things, just enjoying His presence.
verses.illustration_prompt: A father placing a gentle hand on the shoulder of his older son under a star-filled sky, speaking quietly with great tenderness, storybook watercolor style.

verses.kjv: And it was meet that we should make merry, and be glad: for this thy brother was dead, and is alive again; and was lost, and is found.
verses.little_bible: The father said, 'But we had to celebrate and be happy! Your brother was as good as dead — and now he is alive again. He was lost — and now he is found.'
verses.little_reader_adaptation: The father said, 'We must celebrate! Your brother was lost, and now he is found!'
verses.meaning: The father gently corrects the older son — 'your brother,' not 'my son.' Finding the lost is always worth celebrating, and the older son is invited to join the joy.
verses.memory_phrase: He was lost, and now is found.
verses.prayer: God, help me to celebrate every time someone comes back to You, just like the father did.
verses.discussion_question: How does the father's response show us what God's heart is like?
verses.family_discussion: The story ends with the older son outside. What do you think he chose to do? What would you choose?
verses.do_it_today: Go into the celebration — join your family for dinner, a game, or a conversation today with a full heart.
verses.illustration_prompt: The father standing at the open doorway between the older son and the warm, joyful feast inside, arms open to welcome them both, light and music pouring out, storybook watercolor style.
